slides.title: Drinking more water but still *wiped out*?
slides.sub: Water alone isn't hydration. Without electrolytes, a lot of it runs straight through you.

slides.title: They move the water
slides.body: *Sodium, potassium, magnesium* pull water into your cells. No minerals, no real hydration.

slides.title: Cramps, headaches, fog
slides.body: Muscle cramps, afternoon headaches, and *low energy* are classic signs you're watered but not hydrated.

slides.title: Sweat, heat, low-carb
slides.body: Heavy sweating, hot days, and *low-carb eating* all flush sodium faster than you replace it.

slides.title: Add a pinch, eat the food
slides.body: A *pinch of salt* in water plus potassium from fruit and veg covers most people. Skip the sugary sports drinks.

slides.title: Balance, not buckets
slides.body: More isn't better. Most people just need *steady minerals*, not a cabinet of powders.

slides.title: Turn hydration into a *habit you can see*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your water and food together — so you can spot the days you're running low before the headache hits.

caption:
Chugging water and still dragging by 3pm? Water isn't hydration on its own. 💧

Sodium, potassium and magnesium are what pull water into your cells. A pinch of salt plus fruit and veg beats sugary sports drinks for most people. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks your water and food together → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Drinking More Water Isn't Fixing Your Energy
